name: hashlib
description: SHA-1, SHA-256 and MD5 hash algorithms. DO NOT use 'uhashlib' — always import as 'hashlib'.
differences_from_cpython: Only SHA-1, SHA-256 and MD5 are available (CPython has many more: SHA-512, blake2, etc.).

functions.name: hashlib.sha256
functions.description: Create a new SHA-256 hash object.

functions.name: hashlib.sha1
functions.description: Create a new SHA-1 hash object.

functions.name: hashlib.md5
functions.description: Create a new MD5 hash object.
functions.note: MD5 may not be available on all ESP32 firmware builds (depends on mbedtls config).

hash_object_methods.name: update

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Data to add to the hash.

hash_object_methods.description: Add more data to the hash computation.

hash_object_methods.name: digest

returns.description: Hash digest as raw bytes. SHA-256: 32 bytes, MD5: 16 bytes.

hash_object_methods.description: Return the hash digest. Hash object state is NOT reset.

hash_object_methods.name: hexdigest

returns.description: Hash digest as lowercase hexadecimal string.

hash_object_methods.description: Return the hash digest as hex string.

example:
import hashlib

# SHA-256
h = hashlib.sha256(b'hello')
print(h.digest())     # b'...'  (32 bytes)
print(h.hexdigest())  # '2cf24dba...'

# Incremental
h = hashlib.sha256()
h.update(b'hel')
h.update(b'lo')
print(h.hexdigest())  # same as above

# MD5 (if available)
try:
    h = hashlib.md5(b'hello')
    print(h.hexdigest())  # '5d41402abc4b2a76b9719d911017c592'
except AttributeError:
    print('MD5 not available on this firmware')
esp32_notes: SHA-256 is always available (backed by mbedtls).
MD5 may be disabled in some firmware builds to save flash space.
Hash object cannot be reused — you must create a new one after calling digest().
Memory: SHA-256 state is ~208 bytes, MD5 is ~128 bytes.
